zone_6b_notes: Bulletproof perennial for Zone 6b — drought tolerant, heat tolerant, deer resistant, and nearly maintenance-free in Farmington. Thick succulent stems emerge in spring and build slowly to flat-topped, dusty rose-pink flower heads that bloom August–October, then fade to deep copper-bronze through winter. Dried seed heads are beautiful all winter and valued by overwintering beneficial insects. Cut to 4 inches in early spring. Never divide unnecessarily — established clumps are the most attractive. Plant in full sun with excellent drainage; wet clay in winter causes crown rot. Exceptional late-season pollinator plant for monarch butterflies and bees.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant divisions or nursery stock in spring or fall

water: drought tolerant — 0.25–0.5 inch/week; requires excellent drainage
